description: TP=1 enforce-eager max-num-seqs=1 — iter 0 baseline (re-verified on current flake)
drop --enforce-eager → CUDA graphs at BS=1; 2.30x over baseline
ngram spec-decoding (k=5,window=2-4) REGRESSED — likely defeats BS=1 CUDA graphs / verify pass overhead exceeds spec hit gain
kv-cache fp8: within noise of cuda-graphs (Δ -0.94%); simpler wins → discard
VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_MOE_MXFP4_MXFP8=1: MoE intermediate activations FP8 → 3.81x baseline / 1.66x iter-1
compilation pass fusions on top of mxfp8-moe: Δ -2.17% vs iter-4 (within noise but slightly negative); simpler wins
kv-cache fp8 + mxfp8-moe combo: Δ -1.25% vs iter-4 — KV bandwidth still negligible vs MoE matmul at BS=1
VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1: Δ -0.022% vs iter-4 (statistically identical); sampler off the critical path
cudagraph_capture_sizes=[1]: Δ -3.22% vs iter-4 — limiting captures didn't help, slight regression
no-chunked-prefill + no-prefix-cache + no-log-stats: Δ -2.20% vs iter-4 — scheduler overhead wasn't material; chunked-prefill disabled may force slower codepath
CUTLASS variant of MXFP4_MXFP8 MoE: Δ -32.6% vs iter-4 — TRTLLM-gen kernel is decisively faster at BS=1
